channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Bende: N1bn appropriation not for musical instruments alone – Dep Speaker
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Deputy Speaker of the House of Representatives, Benjamin Kalu, has said that the N1 billion appropriation for churches, in Bende Federal Constituency, in the 2026 budget, is not solely for the procurement of musical instruments. The post Bende: N1bn appropriation not for musical instruments al

item.title: Christian persecution: Trump vows to stand with Nigeria in anti-terror war
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: “I applaud your resolve to tackle the issues plaguing your nation, especially the violence affecting Christian communities". The post Christian persecution: Trump vows to stand with Nigeria in anti-terror war appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.dc:creator: BusinessDay Opinion
item.description: The last may not have been heard from the Afrophobic-xenophobic outrages against other African nationals by the South Africans. However,

item.title: 2027: Why Obi-Kwankwaso presidency can’t dialogue with terrorists –Ex-ACF scribe
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A former Secretary-General of the Arewa Consultative Forum (ACF), Mr. Anthony Sani, has said that a proposed administration led by the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC) presidential candidate, Peter Obi and his running mate, Senator Rabiu Kwankwaso, would find it practically impossible to negotiate

item.title: China’s Semight Is Said to Mull Hong Kong Listing After 2,200% Stock Rise
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Semight Instruments Co. is considering a second listing in Hong Kong, people familiar with the matter said, after shares of the Chinese maker of test instruments for semiconductors surged roughly 2,200% since their debut in April.

item.title: Obi-Kwankwaso ticket lacks cross-regional appeal to win presidential election -APC chieftain
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: A leading chieftain of the All Progress Congress (APC) and spokesperson of the Forum of Federal Government Appointees, Illiyasu Musa Kwankwaso has said that the presidential candidate of the National Democratic Congress (NDC), Peter Obi and his running mate, Rabiu Musa Kwankwaso do not possess the r

item.title: FG slashes textbook assessment fees ahead of new ranking policy
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has slashed the fees for the assessment and ranking of textbooks ahead of the implementation of the National Textbook Ranking Policy scheduled to commence in September 2026 The post FG slashes textbook assessment fees ahead of new ranking policy appeared first on The Sun Niger

item.title: Southern Kaduna group disowns video circulating against Senator Katung
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Southern Kaduna Progressives Youth Network has disowned an old video calling for the recall of Senator Sunday Marshall Katung, describing its circulation as misleading and politically outdated, useless and irrelevant. The post Southern Kaduna group disowns video circulating against Senator Katun

item.title: 2026 budget: Atiku, APC trade words over N12.8tn allocation
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Atiku Abubakar and APC trade accusations regarding the N12.8tn Service-Wide Vote in the 2026 budget. Transparency and accountability concerns raised.

item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Rivers State Governor Siminalayi Fubara declares an end to political hostilities, backing President Tinubu and the Wike-led Rainbow Coalition ahead of 2027

item.title: Gov Yusuf inaugurates multi-agency task force on drug abuse, signs executive order against thuggery

item.description: Kano State Governor, Abba Kabir Yusuf, has inaugurated a multi-agency task force on drug abuse. The post Gov Yusuf inaugurates multi-agency task force on drug abuse, signs executive order against thuggery appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Nigerians pay 131% more for Okada fares in three years
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nigerians now pay 131% more for Okada fares than three years ago, with average costs rising to N1,072.51 due to fuel price hikes and naira depreciation.
Read More: https://punchng.com/nigerians-pay-131-more-for-okada-fares-in-three-years/

item.title: Jailed opposition leader Ghannouchi faints in Tunisian prison due to heat
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The incident was witnessed by the daughter of Ahmed Nejib Chebbi, another prominent political prisoner.

item.title: 2027: C’River PDP warns against fake candidates
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Cross River State chapter of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) has warned the public to disregard individuals parading themselves as PDP candidates for the 2027 general election. The post 2027: C’River PDP warns against fake candidates appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Five soldiers killed in southern Thailand checkpoint attack
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The assault in Narathiwat comes a day after a car bombing elsewhere in the province wounded a police officer.

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government must relinquish its dominant role in Nigeria’s energy sector and create an enabling environment for the private sector to lead investment, innovation and operations if the country is to overcome its chronic power crisis. Rabiu Kwankwaso over its presidential candidate in the 2027 general election The post 2027: NNPP’

item.description: The Houthi attacks would be the first in the Red Sea since the Iran-backed group announced a "maritime embargo" against Saudi Arabia.

item.description: Former President Olusegun Obasanjo has urged members of the Ibru family to preserve their unity and build on the values established by the family’s founders, saying doing so would ensure that one of Nigeria’s foremost indigenous business legacies continues to endure across generations. item.title: Nigeria as Federal Republic of Asiwaju? Why not! By Ikechukwu Amaechi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: At the rate some public officials are going, no one should be surprised if a bill surfaces in the National Assembly tomorrow seeking to rename the Federal Republic of Nigeria as the Federal Republic of Asiwaju. When that happens, we will seize being Nigerians and become Asiwajuans! What a great name

item.title: Climate change has intensified Europe's risk of drought, scientists say
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Human-caused climate change has intensified Europe's drought by making extreme heat, rather than low rainfall, the main driver of exceptionally dry conditions, an international team of scientists said on Thursday, warning that a warmer atmosphere is sharply increasing drought and wildfire risks.

item.title: In Gaza, a long path to recovery for one woman who lost eye, and daughter
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Psychological wounds compound Najwa's physical injuries after losing her daughter in an Israeli air strike.

item.title: China Solar Lobby Says Fewer Installs Mark Sustainable Shift
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: China’s solar lobby said the industry is on a more sustainable track, after a policy shift drove a 66% decline in installations in the first half of 2026.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Asian semiconductor stocks rose as Alphabet Inc.’s plans to spend up to $200 billion this year on artificial intelligence computing power refueled optimism over trickle-down benefits.

item.title: People living longer but sicker — Lancet
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: FOR decades, longer life expectancy has been celebrated as one of humanity’s greatest achievements. But a new global study has found that millions of people are surviving longer only to spend more years living with chronic illness, pain and disability. The post People living longer but sicker — Lanc

item.title: Woman Formally Charged Over Karen Carter’s Murder In France
item.dc:creator: TVC News
item.description: French authorities have placed a 70-year-old woman under formal investigation over the fatal stabbing of British-South African mother-of-four Karen Carter in the Dordogne region of France. According to the BBC, Carter, 65, was found with stab wounds beside her car in the village of Trémolat in April

item.title: Dangote refinery resumes petrol sale in naira
item.dc:creator: Daily Trust
item.description: Dangote Petroleum Refinery has resumed gantry loading of Premium Motor Spirit (PMS) in naira after a week-long suspension, while raising its ex-depot petrol price to N1,215 per litre. The refinery had suspended gantry and coastal loading on July 15 after introducing a dollar-denominated pricing temp

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: When fund managers in Taiwan arrive at work each morning, they have to lock away their mobile phones before the stock and bond markets open, and can’t retrieve their devices until the end of the trading day.

item.title: India Silver Imports Slump as New Licensing Disrupts Shipments
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Silver imports into India have slowed sharply as a new licensing regime disrupted shipments, pushing local premiums to a multi-month high.

item.title: Australian Employment Soars in June, Bolstering Rate-Hike Bets
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Australian employment surged in June, extending the previous month’s gains, underscoring the ongoing strength in the nation’s labor market and stoking bets on another interest-rate increase.

item.title: Mexican Mayor Shot Dead in his Office After Surviving Earlier Hit
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Armed assailants gunned down the mayor of a small Mexican city just south of the country’s capital in his office in the latest case of suspected gangland violence targeting a local official.

item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Bill to fund war on Iran and a handful of Trump priorities passes largely along party lines, faces Senate uncertainty.

item.title: ‘Fake’ agency: Tinubu’s amoral rule breeds impunity, abuse of power! By Olu Fasan
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: There is a shocking paradox, a bewildering moral hypocrisy, at the heart of President Bola Tinubu’s leadership. On the one hand, he is actively prosecuting previous public officers for corrupt practices, claiming to have secured over 7,000 convictions since he came to power three years ago. Yet, on

item.title: The bull market faces higher likelihood of a Fed rate hike as Iran crisis intensifies
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Treasury yields are approaching the highest levels seen since the Iran war started in February as oil prices rise

item.title: AI, China, ‘dark factories’ and the future of the US auto industry
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: Economist Mary Lovely dismisses the claims that getting automakers to produce more cars in America proves tariffs work.

item.title: After Orire rescue, what next?
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By SUNNY IKHIOYA The Orire school kidnapping ended on a happy note for the whole country, although a sad one for the family of the slaughtered maths teacher, Oyedokun. Overall, we give thanks; it could have been worse. For once, in a long time, the terrorists’ campaign ended in a total loss for them

item.title: Wildfires ravage Spain, France and Italy, killing three firefighters
item.dc:creator: France 24
item.description: Wildfires swept across southern Europe on Wednesday, killing three firefighters and forcing thousands of people to evacuate as extreme heat and dry conditions fuelled blazes in Sicily, southwestern France and central Spain. Rescue teams battled dozens of fires amid warnings over increasingly severe

item.title: Fast-tracking the National ID project
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: Nigeria should press ahead with finishing its national identity project, and fast. No modern state can govern, plan, or protect a population it cannot properly count. After nearly two decades of fits and starts, the country is finally within sight of getting this right. The numbers tell part of the

item.title: US signs landmark nuclear deal with Saudi Arabia
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US Department of Energy says the "peaceful" co-operation agreement will give US firms "great access" to the Saudi nuclear energy programme.

item.title: Reflections as Ogunwole, FUOYE VC, steps up in age
item.dc:creator: Tribune Opinion
item.description: FEDERAL University Oye-Ekiti (FUOYE) Vice Chancellor, Prof. Joshua Olalekan Ogunwole is a different person to different people, depending on the prisms of perception and shared relationship. The post Reflections as Ogunwole, FUOYE VC, steps up in age appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: ServiceNow’s stock rises as earnings show momentum in cybersecurity
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: Against a gloomy backdrop for software sentiment, ServiceNow just topped revenue expectations.

item.title: IBM just cut its outlook, but not by as much as investors feared
item.dc:creator: MarketWatch
item.description: IBM’s official earnings report comes after a bruising profit warning last week.
